data.title: Meridian3 Industrials prices $175M IPO
data.description:
• Meridian3 Industrials Acquisition (MIACU) priced its initial public offering of 17.5M units at $10/unit, raising $175M.
  • The units are expected to begin trading on Nasdaq on July 2, 2026.
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-half of one redeemable warrant, with each whole warrant exercisable at $11.50/share.
  • The IPO is expected to close on July 6, 2026.
  • The underwriter has a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.625M units to cover over-allotments.

data.title: Oscar Health stock turns "$35.7M insider selling" into a new 52-week high
data.description:
Oscar Health (OSCR) stock rallied on Wednesday after investors digested a newly released SEC Form 4 filing showing CEO and Director Mark T. Bertolini sold 1.24M Class A shares worth about $35.7M.

The Oscar Health stock jumped as much as ~12.23% during the session to a new 52-week high of around $32.06 before closing at ~$31.90, as investors looked past the headline figure and focused on the reason behind the transactions.

The filing showed the transactions were entirely non-discretionary and linked to tax obligations rather than a change in management's view on the company.

According to the Form 4 footnotes, the shares were automatically sold to cover tax withholding obligations related to the deferred settlement of PSUs that vested on April 3, 2026.

The transactions were made under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted in November 2025 and updated in March 2026, indicating the sales were not driven by market timing or concerns about the business.

Investors also viewed the filing as confirmation that the entire $35.7M selling program had already been completed and absorbed by the market, removing a potential overhang on the stock.

On the market side, Oscar Health's latest breakout extended its strong 2026 performance, with the stock up 121.99% year-to-date, far ahead of the 9.60% return delivered by the S&P 500 (SP500).

The stock currently carries a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.92, while Seeking Alpha analysts rate it Buy at 3.75, and Wall Street analysts maintain a Hold rating of 3.18.

data.title: Securitize stock to start trading July 2 after deal with Cantor Equity Partners II closed
data.description:
Securitize completed its previously announced business combination with Cantor Equity Partners II  (CEPT), allowing the tokenization platform's common stock to start trading on the New York Stock Exchange on Thursday, July 2, 2026, under the ticker "SECZ," the companies said Wednesday.

Cantor Equity Partners II (CEPT) stock rose 2.0% in after-hours trading.

Securitize has brought more than $4B in assets onchain. Its public listing will help the firm's platform to further expand.

"Becoming a public company enables us to continue building the infrastructure institutions and investors need as more capital markets move onchain," said Securitize CEO Carlos Domingo.

Since announcing the proposed business combination, Securitize has expanded its institutional platform through collaborations with the New York Stock Exchange (ICE), Computershare, Continental, and other major market participants, while deepening relationships with the world's largest asset managers, including BlackRock (BLK).

data.title: FICO sees Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac's release of Score 10T data as milestone
data.description:
Fair Isaac (FICO) said Wednesday that newly available datasets released by Fannie Mae (FNMA) and Freddie Mac (FMCC) represent a milestone in the mortgage industry's transition to modernized credit scoring.

The release, covering loan-level performance data from April 2013 through September 2025, will enable lenders, investors, and housing finance stakeholders to evaluate the performance of FICO Score 10T using real-world, government-sponsored enterprise loan-level information.

"The Federal Housing Finance Agency and the GSEs have taken an important step forward in advancing the credit score modernization effort with the release of the FICO Score 10T historical data," Julie May, vice president and general manager of B2B Scores at FICO (FICO), said. " We’re eager for market participants to dig into the data to independently validate the strength of the model and see how its use can expand access for borrowers while supporting a more resilient housing finance system."

FICO Score 10T incorporates trended credit data and, when available in the credit bureau file, rental payment history, giving lenders a more complete view of how consumers manage credit and housing payments over time.

FICO® Score 10T is currently available at no cost alongside Classic FICO through the FICO Score 10T Free Access Program, the company said. Almost 70 lenders have signed up for the program.

Fair Isaac (FICO) stock slipped 0.2% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Granite-led JV wins up to $60M contract for Reno bridge replacement project
data.description:
• Granite (GVA) announced on Wednesday that its joint venture, Keystone Bridge Partners, won the preconstruction contract for the Keystone Avenue Bridge Replacement Project in Reno, Nevada.
  • The project will use the Construction Manager at Risk (CMAR) delivery method.
  • The new bridge will replace the existing structurally deficient bridge and improve safety and traffic flow.
  • Additionally, the work includes bridge demolition, construction of a new bridge, road reconstruction, drainage upgrades, utility relocations, and a new multi-use path.
  • The preconstruction work will run from Q2 2026 to Q1 2028, while major construction is planned for Q2 2028 to Q3 2029.
  • The construction phase is expected to be worth $50M-$60M if awarded.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Wednesday after hours: BRX, GBX, CMCO
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes ended lower on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) declining due to technology stocks suffering ongoing volatility.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed -0.2%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq (COMP:IND) ended -0.7%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished flat after having reached a new all-time high mid-session.

Now, here are the three stocks to watch on Wednesday after hours:

  • Brixmor Property Group (BRX) said it acquired four shopping centers for $164.3M as part of its investment activity update for the three and six months ended June 30.
  • Columbus McKinnon (CMCO) appointed John R. Linker as executive vice president of Finance and chief financial officer.
  • Greenbrier (GBX) shares slipped in extended trading hours as its quarterly revenue fell short of the average analyst estimate. Q3 earnings matched the consensus.

data.title: Markets are ‘in waiting more’ as technology stocks struggle, indexes end lower – analyst
data.description:
The stock markets delivered a “humdrum performance” on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) closing down 0.2% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) essentially flat, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) dipped 0.66% as semiconductor companies pulled back.

"It seems as though the market is largely in waiting mode as it seeks additional data for what the future might hold," said Seeking Alpha analyst Daniel Jones. "And, during that time, it is de-risking some of the most expensive companies out there."

The analyst painted a cautious picture of economic conditions, attributing deterioration to inflationary pressures linked to tariffs and the war in Iran.

"If I had to guess, I would say that, before the year is out, we will enter into a stagflationary recession," he said. "And that certainly would not bode well for market participants."

Adding to market uncertainty, new Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh declined to offer forward guidance at the European Central Bank Forum on Central Banking.

"We're going to chart a new course," Warsh said, departing from his predecessor's practice of signaling policy direction.

The Fed chair acknowledged inflation remains too high while emphasizing the central bank's commitment. "We recommit to delivering price stability," he said, adding that anyone expecting the Fed to tolerate above-target inflation "will be disappointed."

The GDPNow model currently projects Q2 2026 real GDP growth at just 1.2%.

Warsh noted that labor markets remain steady and demand solid, though he stressed that getting monetary policy right is "the most important thing we can do."

data.title: Two BlackRock iShares ETFs selected as investment options in Trump Accounts
data.description:
The U.S. Treasury will offer two BlackRock (BLK) iShares ETFs among investment options in Trump Accounts (also known as 530A Accounts).

The ETFs are iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV) and iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF (ITOT).

"IVV and ITOT offer simple, low-cost exposure to the broad U.S. market, making them practical building blocks for long-term investors," said Elise Terry, head of U.S. iShares at BlackRock.

The ETFs are expected to become available through the program in the coming months following the official launch of Trump Accounts.

Trump Accounts are designed to increase long-term savings opportunities for children and families.

"By giving younger Americans the opportunity to start investing earlier, Trump Accounts can help millions build long-term financial security, develop a greater stake in the future of the country, and share more directly in the growth and prosperity of the United States," said CEO Larry Fink.

data.title: Lime rolls into the public market with 6.7M share offering
data.description:
Neutron Holdings (LIME), doing business as Lime, the micromobility operator from San Francisco, California, kicked off its first day as a public company with a 4% gain from its $25 initial public offering price.

The stock opened for trade at $27 per share.

After nearly a decade as a privately owned business, Lime, which rents bicycles and scooters, sold more than 6.6M shares, placing its valuation at $1.66B.

After having flirted with an IPO for at least three years, CEO Wayne Ting found the time was right, made more urgent by “substantial doubts it could continue as a going concern,” a May filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission warned. Without the $167M the IPO raised, Lime would need to raise more funds to address as much as $500M in debt coming due at the end of the year (Lime has nearly $1B in total liabilities).

“Having that resilience and patience and belief and optimism that we will get through the toughest moments has really paid dividends over the long run, because there were many days, weeks, months, where I wasn’t sure if Lime was going to make it past the next three months, four months,” CEO Wayne Ting told TechCrunch in an interview Wednesday.  

The fact that Lime has survived the micromobility business is a testament to its ability to raise cash and generate revenue. The bicycle/scooter rental business is riddled with failures given the high capital costs, severe asset damage, vandalism and theft losses, and shifting urban regulations that plague entrants. Lime competitor Bird filed for bankruptcy in just over two years from its November 2021 IPO, and Helbiz (now operating as Micromobility.com) was delisted from the Nasdaq and never returned as a publicly traded company on a major exchange. Lime even swallowed Jump, future-investor Uber’s own e-bike and scooter business.

Although Lime has managed to expand globally, it owes much of its success to Uber’s (UBER) 24% investment and partnership with the ride-hailing giant. Lime bookings made through Uber accounted for more than 14% of the former’s 2025 revenue.

While the IPO may have given Lime a $167M cash infusion, the company now faces a much more difficult ride. Wall Street will be watching whether it can translate longevity into sustainable earnings growth as a public company.
